alt_forms.note: Sports-register equivalent; sounds too athletic for board/miniature wargame contexts.

definitions.gloss_en: a person taking part in a game; the human decision-maker behind a side
definitions.gloss_jp: ゲームに参加する人；陣営を操作する人間
definitions.explanation_en: The agent controlling one side in a game. Distinct from in-game characters or units, which are what the player controls.
definitions.implicit_notes: In miniature wargames, プレイヤー refers to the person, not their army. Rulebooks use it to address the reader directly.
definitions.orthography_notes: Katakana loanword from English 'player'. No standard kanji form exists in the gaming sense. The docx notes 選手 (athlete) as an inappropriate substitute.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Universal across all Japanese game genres. Warhammer Japan also uses it but did not originate the term. Fire Emblem (Nintendo, 1990–present) and Famicom Wars (Nintendo, 1988–present) use it independently.

usage_jp: プレイヤーは{手|て}{番|ばん}に{行|こう}{動|どう}する (the player acts on their turn); {手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤー (active player)
usage_en: "the player" or "you" when rulebooks address the reader.
comparative_notes: 選手 is the correct word in sports contexts but reads as too competitive and physical for tabletop usage. プレイヤー is the universally accepted loanword.

examples.japanese: {手|て}{番|ばん}プレイヤーが{行|こう}{動|どう}を{選|えら}ぶ。
examples.english: The active player chooses an action.

cross_references.note: 手番プレイヤー — the active player compound
非手番プレイヤー — the reactive player compound
